info.title: VulnerableCode API
info.description:
VulnerableCode is open data and free software by nexB Inc. and others.

The VulnerableCode API exposes these endpoints:

  • packages/: main endpoint to lookup for vulnerable packages.
  • vulnerabilities/: secondary endpoint to lookup by vulnerabilities.
  • alias/: secondary endpoint to lookup vulnerabilities by aliases (e.g., CVE)
  • cpes/: secondary endpoint to lookup vulnerabilities by CPE.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

get.description: Return a list of Package URLs of vulnerable packages.

post.description: Return the response for exact PackageURLs requested for.
Lookup for vulnerable packages using many Package URLs at once.

To use it, override the class and set the `.queryset` and
`.serializer_class` attributes.

scoring_system.description:
Identifier for the scoring system used. Available choices are: cvssv2: CVSSv2 Base Score,
cvssv3: CVSSv3 Base Score,
cvssv3.1: CVSSv3.1 Base Score,
cvssv4: CVSSv4 Base Score,
rhbs: RedHat Bugzilla severity,
rhas: RedHat Aggregate severity,
archlinux: Archlinux Vulnerability Group Severity,
cvssv3.1_qr: CVSSv3.1 Qualitative Severity Rating,
generic_textual: Generic textual severity rating,
apache_httpd: Apache Httpd Severity,
apache_tomcat: Apache Tomcat Severity,
epss: Exploit Prediction Scoring System,
ssvc: Stakeholder-Specific Vulnerability Categorization,
openssl: OpenSSL Severity,
ubuntu-priority: Ubuntu Priority

scoring_elements.description: Supporting scoring elements used to compute the score values. For example a CVSS vector string as used to compute a CVSS score.

published_at.description: UTC Date of publication of the vulnerability severity

url.description: Link to the advisory on the upstream website

exploitability.description: Exploitability indicates the likelihood that a vulnerability in a software package could be used by malicious actors to compromise systems, applications, or networks. This metric is determined automatically based on the discovery of known exploits.

weighted_severity.description: Weighted severity is the highest value calculated by multiplying each severity by its corresponding weight, divided by 10.

risk_score.description: Risk expressed as a number ranging from 0 to 10. Risk is calculated from weighted severity and exploitability values. It is the maximum value of (the weighted severity multiplied by its exploitability) or 10. Risk = min(weighted severity * exploitability, 10)

url.description: Link to the advisory on the upstream website

Alias.description: Used for nesting inside package focused APIs.

alias.description: An alias is a unique vulnerability identifier in some database, such as CVE-2020-2233

CodeFix.description:
Serializer for the CodeFix model.
Provides detailed information about a code fix.

commits.description: List of commit identifiers using VCS URLs associated with the code change.

pulls.description: List of pull request URLs associated with the code change.

downloads.description: List of download URLs for the patched code.

patch.description: The code change as a patch in unified diff format.

fixed_package_purl.description: PURL of the fixing package (if available).

notes.description: Notes or instructions about this code change.

references.description: URL references related to this code change.

is_reviewed.description: Indicates if this code change has been reviewed.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the code fix was created.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the code fix was last updated.

CodeFixV2.description:
Serializer for the CodeFix model.
Provides detailed information about a code fix.

commits.description: List of commit identifiers using VCS URLs associated with the code change.

pulls.description: List of pull request URLs associated with the code change.

downloads.description: List of download URLs for the patched code.

patch.description: The code change as a patch in unified diff format.

affected_advisory_id.description: ID of the advisory affecting the package.

fixed_package_purl.description: PURL of the fixing package (if available).

notes.description: Notes or instructions about this code change.

references.description: URL references related to this code change.

is_reviewed.description: Indicates if this code change has been reviewed.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the code fix was created.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the code fix was last updated.

date_added.description: The date the vulnerability was added to an exploit catalog.

description.description: Description of the vulnerability in an exploit catalog, often a refinement of the original CVE description

required_action.description: The required action to address the vulnerability, typically to apply vendor updates or apply vendor mitigations or to discontinue use.

due_date.description: The date the required action is due, which applies to all USA federal civilian executive branch (FCEB) agencies, but all organizations are strongly encouraged to execute the required action

notes.description: Additional notes and resources about the vulnerability, often a URL to vendor instructions.

known_ransomware_campaign_use.description:
Known' if this vulnerability is known to have been leveraged as part of a ransomware campaign; 
        or 'Unknown' if there is no confirmation that the vulnerability has been utilized for ransomware.

source_date_published.description: The date that the exploit was published or disclosed.

exploit_type.description: The type of the exploit as provided by the original upstream data source.

platform.description: The platform associated with the exploit as provided by the original upstream data source.

source_date_updated.description: The date the exploit was updated in the original upstream data source.

data_source.description: The source of the exploit information, such as CISA KEV, exploitdb, metaspoit, or others.

source_url.description: The URL to the exploit as provided in the original upstream data source.

MinimalPackage.description: Used for nesting inside vulnerability focused APIs.

Package.description: Lookup software package using Package URLs

type.description: A short code to identify the type of this package. For example: gem for a Rubygem, docker for a container, pypi for a Python Wheel or Egg, maven for a Maven Jar, deb for a Debian package, etc.

namespace.description: Package name prefix, such as Maven groupid, Docker image owner, GitHub user or organization, etc.

subpath.description: Extra subpath within a package, relative to the package root.

risk_score.description: Risk score between 0.00 and 10.00, where higher values indicate greater vulnerability risk for the package.

purls.description: List of PackageURL strings in canonical form.
List of PackageURL strings in canonical form.

is_active.description: When set to True, this Pipeline is active. When set to False, this Pipeline is inactive and not run.

live_logging.description: When enabled logs will be streamed live during pipeline execution. For legacy importers and improvers, logs are always made available only after execution completes.

run_interval.description: Number of hours to wait between run of this pipeline.

execution_timeout.description: Number hours before pipeline execution is forcefully terminated.

schedule_work_id.description: Identifier used to manage the periodic run job.
